intro: Haloragis depressa, also known as swamp dodder, is a creeping, perennial plant native to Australia and New Zealand. The health benefits of Haloragis depressa include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as skin irritations and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
